summarization.a: The chart shows a linear decrease in mass change over time at 300°C on Al₂O₃, with sequence of TMA and HF treatments indicated in the figure.

a.question: Does the mass change increase with increasing time/duration?
What is the ALE etching sequence ?
a.answer:
One ALE cycle consists of 
I. TMA dose of 2.0 s
II.  N2 purge of 30 s
III. HF dose of 1.0 s
IV. N2 purge of 30 s.

a.question: Does all ALE cycles lead to a decrease in mass?
a.answer: All ALE cycles show mass loss except during the first ALE cycle. The first cycle displays mass gains.

a.question: What does the slope of Mass change give?
a.answer:
slope of
the mass change versus time yields a mass change per cycle
(MCPC) of −15.9 ng/(cm2 cycle). This MCPC represents the
removal of 9.4 × 1013 “Al2O3” units/(cm2 cycle). This MCPC is
also equivalent to an etch rate of 0.51 Å/cycle based on the
Al2O3 ALD film density of 3.1 g/cm3 determined by XRR.
